activities_projects.title: Detailed River Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the course, tributaries, and uses of a major river
steps: Choose a major river in India
Research its source, tributaries, and length
Note how people use the river for irrigation, transport, and drinking water
Create a detailed diagram showing river course and major uses
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain detailed knowledge of river systems and their significance
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness, accuracy, and diagram clarity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Water Quality Testing
activities_projects.objective: Analyze water quality and detect impurities
materials: Samples of water from different sources
steps: Collect water from tap, river, and pond
Observe color, smell, and clarity
Test pH using litmus paper
Record findings and compare sources
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand water quality variations and pollution indicators

activities_projects.title: Rainwater Harvesting Design Plan
activities_projects.objective: Plan a simple rainwater harvesting system
steps: Study your school or home layout
Draw a rainwater harvesting system including collection, filtration, and storage
Label all components and describe their functions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn practical ways to collect and conserve rainwater

activities_projects.title: Impact of Water Scarcity Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand effects of water scarcity on communities
steps: Select a region in India affected by water scarcity
Research causes, effects, and solutions implemented
Write a case study with 3 major impacts and suggested solutions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand water scarcity challenges and management efforts

activities_projects.title: Groundwater Level Observation
activities_projects.objective: Analyze groundwater availability in your area
materials: Local borewell or water table data (if available)
steps: Collect data on groundwater depth from local sources
Compare with past records (if available)
Write observations on seasonal changes and human impact
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how groundwater levels fluctuate and factors affecting them
activities_projects.evaluation: Data collection accuracy and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study: Traditional vs Modern Water Management
activities_projects.objective: Compare traditional water conservation methods with modern techniques
steps: List traditional methods (step wells, tanks, ponds) and modern methods (rainwater harvesting, dams)
Compare effectiveness, cost, and sustainability
Create a table summarizing differences and advantages
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand evolution of water management methods

activities_projects.title: Local Water Body Observation Report
activities_projects.objective: Observe human impact on a nearby water body
steps: Visit a local river, lake, or pond
Observe pollution, usage, and surrounding activities
Write a report suggesting ways to protect and maintain it
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect classroom knowledge with local environmental issues
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation detail and practical suggestions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Water Footprint Calculation
steps: List daily water uses including drinking, cooking, bathing, and washing
Calculate approximate liters used per day
Reflect on ways to reduce water footprint and write a short plan
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand individual responsibility in water conservation

activities_projects.title: Poster: Effects of Water Pollution
activities_projects.objective: Illustrate the consequences of water pollution on humans and wildlife
steps: Choose one type of water pollution (chemical, plastic, sewage)
Show effects on humans, animals, and plants
Add suggestions to reduce pollution
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand environmental impact of polluted water
activities_projects.evaluation: Visual clarity, creativity, and accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mini Field Survey: Water Usage Patterns
activities_projects.objective: Analyze water usage patterns in households
steps: Prepare a 5-question survey on household water usage
Interview at least 5 households
Analyze data and present results in a table or chart
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain understanding of real-life water consumption trends
activities_projects.evaluation: Survey design, data collection, and presentation (10 marks)
